title: Add signal proxying to podman run and attach
body:
Also removes sig-proxy from 'podman create', where is does not make sense.

Fixes #372

comments.body: Could probably use some tests, but it's also the weekend. I'll get to it sometime on monday.
Note to self: does handling SIGWINCH here conflict with #448 - investigate further
Wouldn't you need it in podman start then?  I thought the podman create was just to make sure that the sig-proxy was setup for start?
@rhatdan I don't see `--sig-proxy` in the `docker create` manpage. If we want it in start, I would not be opposed to adding the flag there, though, that's easy enough
Answering myself from earlier - Go will forward copies of signals independently to separate channels from `signal.Notify()` so it will not interfere with #448
I guess it would have to go into podman start --sig-proxy, then.  Not sure why you would do run and attach without start?
Docker doesn't have it, but there's no technical reason we can't do it for start --attach. I'll add it in.

Added sig-proxy to `podman start` (though flipped from default-true to default-false, because it conflicts with starting multiple containers). Also added integration tests, which are hopefully passing.
Sig-proxy seems broken on travis. Will investigate tomorrow.
Rebased & force-pushed, let's see if Travis passes now
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 99b9595) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
This fails in containers. Still trying to identify the reason
Seems to have passed tests now?
Guess it could be a flaky test
